---
name: New finding (ruling or statute change)
about: Propose a new BGH / ECJ / OLG / LG ruling, statute change, or DSK position paper to add to findings/_current.md
title: "find: [court] [date] [short topic] (e.g. 'find: BGH 12.03.2026 cookies')"
labels: ["new-finding", "needs-verification"]
---

## Citation (verbatim)

> Court, Urteil v. DD.MM.YYYY, AZ (e.g. "BGH, Urteil v. 12.03.2026, VI ZR 23/23")

## Primary source URL (Tier 1)

<!-- Required. ONE of: bundesgerichtshof.de press/judgment, openjur.de, curia.europa.eu, eur-lex.europa.eu, gesetze-im-internet.de, the LfDI's own publication page. -->

URL: 

## Holding (1-3 sentences)

<!-- What did the court decide? Avoid paraphrasing the lawyer's headline; describe what the operative paragraph actually says. -->

## Proposed Abmahn-relevance score

- [ ] HIGH — static-scrapable browser-side pattern (e.g. Google Fonts CDN, missing § 5 DDG header, "Kündigungswunsch" button label)
- [ ] MEDIUM — requires runtime analysis or some context
- [ ] LOW — requires per-instance evidence
- [ ] DEFENSIVE — this ruling NARROWS an obligation or gives defendants a shield

<!-- Check the relevant `skills/audit/checks/phase-N-*.md`. List which phase(s) should reference this finding. -->

- [ ] Phase 1 — static resources
- [ ] Phase 2 — runtime Playwright
- [ ] Phase 3 — legal docs
- [ ] Phase 4 — sub-processors
- [ ] Phase 5 — music-library copyright
- [ ] Phase 6 — image attribution
- [ ] Phase 7 — e-commerce
- [ ] Phase 8 — AI Act Art. 50

## Proposed lifecycle status

- [ ] active
- [ ] pending-higher-court (referral pending, lower courts may stay)
- [ ] defensive
- [ ] imminent (firm future date — include below)

## Verification status

- [ ] I have read the primary source linked above (not just the press release)
- [ ] The ruling is final (no pending appeal that would change the holding)
- [ ] This is a German / EU jurisdiction ruling
